summary:
The recent RCT showed no reduction in major CV events from omega-3 supplementation, and although beneficial in managing high triglycerides, omega-3s lack consistent preventive effects against cardiac mortality. Thus, current evidence does not validate the Omega-3 Index as an independent predictor of coronary heart disease-related deaths.  

(Word count: 68)

We are only allowed two sentences. We must focus strictly on clinically significant findings related to whether the Omega-3 Index predicts CHD death.

Final summary:

A large randomized trial found no benefit of omega-3 supplements in reducing cardiovascular events, undermining their role as a predictive marker for coronary heart disease death. Current evidence does not establish the Omega-3 Index as an independent risk factor for death from coronary heart disease.
